prompt_pipeline.system_prompt:
You are a chess engine playing Three-Player Chess on a 96-cell hexagonal board.

BOARD: Regular hexagon, 96 cells. Columns A–L, rows 1–12.
Three segments of 32 cells:
- White: A–H rows 1–4. Black: A–D+I–L rows 5–8. Red: E–H+I–L rows 9–12.
- Invalid: E5–H8, I1–L4, A9–D12.

BRIDGES (columns connecting 2 segments):
- A–D: White↔Black. E–H: White↔Red. I–L: Black↔Red.

STARTING POSITION:
- White row 1: A1=R B1=N C1=B D1=Q E1=K F1=B G1=N H1=R. Pawns row 2.
- Black row 8: A8=R B8=N C8=B D8=Q I8=K J8=B K8=N L8=R. Pawns row 7.
- Red row 12: H12=R G12=N F12=B E12=Q I12=K J12=B K12=N L12=R. Pawns row 11.

TURN ORDER: White → Black → Red (clockwise).

PIECES: Standard chess moves adapted to hex geometry.
- Pawns move forward along their column toward the opponent's base row.
- Pawns capture diagonally forward (up to 3 directions at triple junctions: D4,E4,D5,I5,E9,I9).
- Double pawn move from starting row. En passant. Promotion on opponent's base row.
- Castling along base row (O-O short, O-O-O long).

SPECIAL: When a player is checkmated, their pieces (except king) transfer to the mating player.
WIN: Checkmate both opponents.

You are a strong player. Analyze the position carefully before choosing a move.
Prioritize: king safety > material > center control > development.
prompt_pipeline.rendered_system:
You are a chess engine playing Three-Player Chess on a 96-cell hexagonal board.

BOARD: Regular hexagon, 96 cells. Columns A–L, rows 1–12.
Three segments of 32 cells:
- White: A–H rows 1–4. Black: A–D+I–L rows 5–8. Red: E–H+I–L rows 9–12.
- Invalid: E5–H8, I1–L4, A9–D12.

BRIDGES (columns connecting 2 segments):
- A–D: White↔Black. E–H: White↔Red. I–L: Black↔Red.

STARTING POSITION:
- White row 1: A1=R B1=N C1=B D1=Q E1=K F1=B G1=N H1=R. Pawns row 2.
- Black row 8: A8=R B8=N C8=B D8=Q I8=K J8=B K8=N L8=R. Pawns row 7.
- Red row 12: H12=R G12=N F12=B E12=Q I12=K J12=B K12=N L12=R. Pawns row 11.

TURN ORDER: White → Black → Red (clockwise).

PIECES: Standard chess moves adapted to hex geometry.
- Pawns move forward along their column toward the opponent's base row.
- Pawns capture diagonally forward (up to 3 directions at triple junctions: D4,E4,D5,I5,E9,I9).
- Double pawn move from starting row. En passant. Promotion on opponent's base row.
- Castling along base row (O-O short, O-O-O long).

SPECIAL: When a player is checkmated, their pieces (except king) transfer to the mating player.
WIN: Checkmate both opponents.

You are a strong player. Analyze the position carefully before choosing a move.
Prioritize: king safety > material > center control > development.

TRIUMVIRATE POSITION FORMAT:

Regular cells: [Sector][Ring]/[Opponent][Depth].[Flank]
  Sector: W = White, B = Black, R = Red.
  Ring (1-3): Distance from board center. 1 = inner zone, 3 = edge.
  Opponent: Which enemy sector this cell faces (W/B/R).
  Depth (0-3): Steps to front line (row 4). 0 = front line, 3 = rear.
  Flank (0-3): Steps to border with that opponent. 0 = at border, 3 = center.

Rosette cells: C/[Source].[Neighbor]
  C = Center (rosette, ring 0). Strongest position on board.
  Source: Sector with direct access to this cell (W/B/R).
  Neighbor: Second adjacent sector (W/B/R).

Reading examples:
  W2/R1.2 = White sector, ring 2, facing Red, depth 1, flank 2.
  C/W.B = Center rosette, accessible from White, bordering Black.

STRATEGY: Lower numbers = stronger. Buried level = Ring + Depth, keep below 4.

Pieces: L = Leader (King), M = Marshal (Queen), T = Train (Rook),
        D = Drone (Bishop), N = Noctis (Knight), P = Private (Pawn).

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Respond with a JSON object only (no markdown):
{"thinking": "your analysis", "move_from": "W2/R2.3", "move_to": "C/W.R"}
For promotion: {"thinking": "...", "move_from": "W2/R1.3", "move_to": "W3/R0.3", "promotion": "queen"}
Use the thinking field to reason step by step before deciding.
ONLY use moves from the legal moves list.
prompt_pipeline.rendered_user_prompt:
Position (3PF): 
Your color: WHITE
Move number: 6
Last move: C/R.W → C/B.W (capture)

Legal moves:

  W3/B3.1: W1/B1.2, W2/B2.3
  W3/B3.2: W2/B2.3
  W3/R3.3: W2/R2.3, W3/B3.3

Choose the best move. Reply with exactly: FROM TO
Example: E2 E4
For promotion, add =PIECE: A7 A8=Q

Board:
  BLACK: BB3/R3.2 BW1/B1.2 KB3/R3.3 NB3/R3.1 NB3/W3.1 PB1/W1.2 PB2/R2.1 PB2/R2.2 PB2/R2.3 PB2/W2.1 PB3/R0.0 PB3/W2.0 QB3/W3.3 RB3/R3.0 RB3/W3.0
  RED: BR2/W2.3 BR3/B3.2 KR3/B3.3 NR3/B3.1 NR3/W3.1 PC/B.W PR2/B2.1 PR2/B2.2 PR2/B2.3 PR2/W2.1 PR2/W2.2 PR3/B2.0 PR3/W0.0 QR3/W3.3 RR3/B3.0 RR3/W3.0
  WHITE ← YOU: BW3/B3.2 BW3/R3.2 KW3/R3.3 NW3/B3.1 NW3/R3.1 PC/W.B PC/W.R PW2/B2.1 PW2/B2.2 PW2/R2.1 PW2/R2.2 PW3/B2.0 PW3/R2.0 RW3/B3.0 RW3/R3.0

messages.content:
You are a chess engine playing Three-Player Chess on a 96-cell hexagonal board.

- White: A–H rows 1–4. Black: A–D+I–L rows 5–8. Red: E–H+I–L rows 9–12.

STARTING POSITION:
- White row 1: A1=R B1=N C1=B D1=Q E1=K F1=B G1=N H1=R. Pawns row 2.
- Black row 8: A8=R B8=N C8=B D8=Q I8=K J8=B K8=N L8=R. Pawns row 7.
- Red row 12: H12=R G12=N F12=B E12=Q I12=K J12=B K12=N L12=R. Pawns row 11.

TURN ORDER: White → Black → Red (clockwise).

PIECES: Standard chess moves adapted to hex geometry.
- Pawns move forward along their column toward the opponent's base row.
- Pawns capture diagonally forward (up to 3 directions at triple junctions: D4,E4,D5,I5,E9,I9).
- Double pawn move from starting row. En passant. Promotion on opponent's base row.
- Castling along base row (O-O short, O-O-O long).

SPECIAL: When a player is checkmated, their pieces (except king) transfer to the mating player.
WIN: Checkmate both opponents.

You are a strong player. Analyze the position carefully before choosing a move.
Prioritize: king safety > material > center control > development.

TRIUMVIRATE POSITION FORMAT:

Regular cells: [Sector][Ring]/[Opponent][Depth].[Flank]
  Sector: W = White, B = Black, R = Red.
  Ring (1-3): Distance from board center. 1 = inner zone, 3 = edge.
  Opponent: Which enemy sector this cell faces (W/B/R).
  Depth (0-3): Steps to front line (row 4). 0 = front line, 3 = rear.
  Flank (0-3): Steps to border with that opponent. 0 = at border, 3 = center.

Rosette cells: C/[Source].[Neighbor]
  C = Center (rosette, ring 0). Strongest position on board.
  Source: Sector with direct access to this cell (W/B/R).
  Neighbor: Second adjacent sector (W/B/R).

Reading examples:
  W2/R1.2 = White sector, ring 2, facing Red, depth 1, flank 2.
  C/W.B = Center rosette, accessible from White, bordering Black.

STRATEGY: Lower numbers = stronger. Buried level = Ring + Depth, keep below 4.

Pieces: L = Leader (King), M = Marshal (Queen), T = Train (Rook),
        D = Drone (Bishop), N = Noctis (Knight), P = Private (Pawn).

OUTPUT FORMAT:

For promotion: {"thinking": "...", "move_from": "W2/R1.3", "move_to": "W3/R0.3", "promotion": "queen"}
Use the thinking field to reason step by step before deciding.
ONLY use moves from the legal moves list.

Your color: WHITE
Move number: 6
Last move: C/R.W → C/B.W (capture)

Legal moves:
  W2/B2.1: W1/B1.2
  W3/B3.1: W1/B1.2, W2/B2.3
  W3/B3.2: W2/B2.3
  W3/R3.3: W2/R2.3, W3/B3.3

Choose the best move. Reply with exactly: FROM TO
Example: E2 E4
For promotion, add =PIECE: A7 A8=Q

Board:
  BLACK: BB3/R3.2 BW1/B1.2 KB3/R3.3 NB3/R3.1 NB3/W3.1 PB1/W1.2 PB2/R2.1 PB2/R2.2 PB2/R2.3 PB2/W2.1 PB3/R0.0 PB3/W2.0 QB3/W3.3 RB3/R3.0 RB3/W3.0
  RED: BR2/W2.3 BR3/B3.2 KR3/B3.3 NR3/B3.1 NR3/W3.1 PC/B.W PR2/B2.1 PR2/B2.2 PR2/B2.3 PR2/W2.1 PR2/W2.2 PR3/B2.0 PR3/W0.0 QR3/W3.3 RR3/B3.0 RR3/W3.0

llm_responses.raw_response: {"thinking": "The last move was a capture at the center rosette, increasing tension. White's king is at KW3/R3.3, relatively safe but needs support. Among legal moves, moving BW3/B3.2 to W1/B1.2 advances a bishop towards the center, increasing control and potential pressure on Black's side. Other moves either retreat or don't improve position significantly. Prioritizing center control and development, I choose BW3/B3.2 to W1/B1.2.", "move_from": "W3/B3.2", "move_to": "W1/B1.2"}

{"thinking": "The last move was a capture by Black on the center rosette C/B.W, which means Black is active centrally. For White, maintaining central control and king safety is crucial. Among the legal moves, moving the knight from W3/R3.2 to W1/B1.2 places pressure on Black's forward positions and also develops the knight closer to the center. This also supports White's control over key hexes facing Black. Other moves are less central or less aggressive. Therefore, moving the knight from W3/R3.2 to W1/B1.2 is the best choice to strengthen White's position and prepare for further central control and defense.", "move_from": "W3/R3.2", "move_to": "W1/B1.2"}

{"thinking": "The last move was a capture on the center rosette C/B.W, so controlling this strong central cell is crucial. I see that BW3/B3.2 can move to W1/B1.2, a forward position close to the center, or W2/B2.3, a slightly safer but more defensive spot. W3/B3.1 can also move to W1/B1.2, contesting the center more aggressively. Among the options, moving W3/B3.1 to W1/B1.2 puts pressure on the black pieces near the center and helps control the front lines, improving development and center control without exposing the king or losing material. Therefore, I choose to move W3/B3.1 to W1/B1.2.", "move_from": "W3/B3.1", "move_to": "W1/B1.2"}
